experiences.company: Data Science Student Society at UC San Diego
experiences.title: Software Lead
description: Leading a team of developers in designing and building full-stack websites for UC San Diego student organizations, using modern web technologies and agile practices.

Organize and deliver workshops on essential software engineering topics, including frontend frameworks, backend architecture, Git/GitHub, containerization, and cloud computing, to help students gain hands-on experience and develop industry-ready skills.

experiences.company: Data Science Student Society at UC San Diego
experiences.title: Software Developer
description: Built responsive frontend features for club websites using React.
Led a workshop on developing a personal portfolio site, guiding students through design, layout, and deployment best practices.
Collaborated with a cross-functional team to deliver clean, user-friendly interfaces for student organizations

experiences.title: Data Engineer Intern
description: Deployed containerized cloud-based web scraper to save $200k+ in operating expenses
Operationalized AI models to transform patient data into study-specific narratives
Deployed scalable data pipelines with AWS serverless functions and Power BI dashboards

experiences.title: Data Science Intern
description: Implementing a social media web scraper to gather data to perform sentiment analysis on current county policies. The goal is to make a public tool for SD Taxpayers and policymakers to see sentiments towards certain policies.

experiences.title: Code Coach
description: Led 4 week-long summer camps, teaching Python fundamentals to over 50 students ages 6–17 through hands-on projects and games.
Provided 1-on-1 tutoring in Lua for Roblox game development, helping students apply programming concepts to interactive, real-world applications.
Fostered a fun and supportive learning environment that encouraged curiosity, problem-solving, and creativity in young coders.

projects.title: Portfolio Website
description: Designed and developed my portfolio website utilizing React and React Three Fiber, featuring an engaging 3D Boids Simulation on the landing page. The site comprises eight pages, effectively showcasing my projects and skills. I invite you to explore my work and experience the interactive elements firsthand.

projects.title: YipYap: The Chat App for Yappers
description: Designed and developed YipYap, a real-time chat application for "Yappers" - those who love to talk. YipYap includes features like AI-generated topic suggestions and personalized chat groups, aiming to foster more dynamic conversations.

Utilizing the MERN stack—MongoDB, Express, React, and Node.js—I implemented real-time communication through SocketIO. I enhanced state management with Zustand and improved the user interface using Tailwind CSS and DaisyUI. This project significantly advanced my skills in full-stack development, API creation, RESTful services, database management, and user authentication.

projects.title: Sorting Visualizer
description: Developed a sorting algorithm visualizer for 7 different sorting algorithms to help beginners understand the mechanics of each. Implemented interactive features, allowing users to modify settings such as algorithm parallelization (e.g., Quicksort, Mergesort) and view performance metrics like time taken, swaps, and comparisons.

projects.title: Wave Simulation
description: Implemented 1D and 2D wave simulations using the Finite-Difference Method, applying Vector Calculus concepts for four distinct boundary conditions. Authored and published research findings in a Medium article, showcasing computational results and visualization.

projects.title: PyTorch Digit Recognizer
description: Enhanced the previous digit recognizer by implementing the PyTorch library, boosting accuracy from 87% to 93% on the MNIST dataset. Reduced training time by 10x through optimization techniques and hyperparameter tuning to further improve model efficiency and performance.

projects.title: Dino Classifier
description: Associated with UC San Diego

Ranked 3rd out of 30 teams at UCSD DataHacks by developing a machine learning model that predicts dinosaur traits such as lifespan and speed using modern animal data. Managed code collaboration with GitHub for version control, ensuring smooth teamwork and continuous integration.

Cleaned and visualized datasets using Pandas and Matplotlib to analyze dinosaur characteristics. Presented findings in a live demo, showcasing the model’s practical applications and key insights for the competition audience.

projects.title: Digit Recognizer
description: Built a handwritten digit recognizer from scratch using only Numpy, linear algebra, and multivariable calculus, achieving 87% accuracy on the MNIST dataset. Developed custom data visualizations to fine-tune hyperparameters and optimize model performance without relying on external machine learning libraries.
